title: Le passé composé (être) — Movement verbs
objective: Narrate past events involving movement, with correct agreement (A2: can recount a journey or trip).

bridge.title: Bridge from Hindi / English: with être, the participle agrees with the subject
bridge.body: A small set of movement/state verbs (aller, venir, partir, rester…) form the passé composé with **être**, and the past participle then **agrees** with the subject: *il est allé*, *elle est allé**e***, *ils sont allé**s***, *elles sont allé**es***. English has no such agreement, but Hindi does — वह गया (he went) vs वह गई (she went). Lean on that instinct; just add -e (feminine) and -s (plural) to the participle.

example.french: Je suis allé à Goa en train.
example.englishGloss: I went to Goa by train.

example.french: Ils sont partis à six heures.
Pour Diwali, Arjun est rentré à Jaipur.
example.englishGloss: For Diwali, Arjun returned home to Jaipur.

cards.englishGloss: to stay (NOT to rest) — past participle: resté

example.french: Le voyage à Goa était long.
example.englishGloss: The trip to Goa was long.

exercises.hint: être verb → participle agrees: feminine adds -e.

exercises.prompt: Which auxiliary do movement verbs like « aller » use in the passé composé?
exercises.explanation: Movement/state verbs (aller, venir, partir…) use être; the participle then agrees with the subject.

exercises.prompt: Passé composé of « aller »: Nous ___ à Delhi. (two words)
